text:
REGIME — Small caps led, defensives rotated in, discretionary lagged.

INDICES — VIX 23.9, down on the day. IWM +0.69%, QQQ +0.11%, SPY +0.09%, DIA -0.09%; small caps outperformed large caps across the board. USO surged +11.15%, the dominant cross-asset move on the session.

SECTORS — XLRE +1.61%, XLK +0.80%, XLP +0.53%, XLU +0.50%, XLE +0.47%, XLC +0.41%, XLF +0.18%, XLB -0.10%, XLI -0.40%, XLV -0.62%, XLY -1.50%. Top industries: USO +11.15% and MSOS +4.62%; bottom: TAN -2.52% and SLV -3.45%.

THE 7s — Toll Road +1.64%, Semi +1.27%, Energy +0.91%, New Metals +0.87%, New Staples +0.83%, Cloud +0.75%, Defense +0.55%, Software +0.54%, Old Staples +0.50%, Insurance +0.32%, Buildout +0.19%, Freight +0.18%, Bank +0.15%, Power +0.04%, Medtech +0.00%, Old Metals -0.21%, Housing -0.71%, Health -0.76%, Industrial -0.92%, Fab 7 -1.21%. Strongest ticker in Toll Road not specified in data; weakest in Fab 7 drove that basket's -1.21% drag.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 51.2, Neutral. Top movers: USO +11.15% (cross-asset/energy), MSOS +4.62% (industries), SLV -3.45% (cross-asset/metals). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +5.59%, worst XLY -7.02%; best basket Energy +8.04%, worst Software -8.35%. USO's +11.15% spike ties directly to geopolitical risk premium from U.S. strikes on Iran despite broader peace-resolution optimism.

WATCH — USO: +11.15%, one-day vol extreme on geopolitical catalyst; watch for mean reversion or follow-through as Iran headline risk remains binary. IJT: +0.53% on 2.6x average volume, small-cap vol spike warrants monitoring for trend confirmation or fade. XLY: -1.50%, worst sector, negative 20d at -7.02% and weak 5d; discretionary deterioration accelerating relative to broader tape recovery.
